div.head: PE–38. Editorial Note
div.p:
Airgram 97 to Lima, November 10, 1959, authorized the Embassy in Peru to negotiate with the Peruvian Government for the sale of $12 million (including one half the cost of transportation) worth of United States surplus agricultural commodities under Title I of Public Law 480, the Agricultural Trade development and Assistance Act of 1954. Enclosed was a draft sales agreement. (411.2341/11-1059) The proposed agreement was signed at Lima on February 12, 1960. Of the total value of approximately $12 million, 45 percent was to be loaned to Peru for economic development; 25 percent to finance loans to private enterprises in Peru under Section 104(e), the Cooley Amendment to the Act; and 30 percent was to be retained for United States Government
use. For the text of the agreement, see 11 UST 186. For Section 104(e), which provided that up to 25 percent of local currency from Title I sales he made available for loans to U.S. and to private investors through the Export-Import Bank, see 71 State 345.
